hypothesis: Pairing each actionable-now routing tag with a specific 1-sentence suggested action (e.g., '→ dev: evaluate agentmemory repo as cortextOS memory layer replacement, start with architecture section') converts boss and specialist triage from routing decisions to single-step execution, pushing adoptions above the cycle-2 high of 9. Evidence: cycle-4 DISCARD (7 adoptions) showed routing tags removed triage overhead but left adoption conversion low because recipients received the item but no concrete next action. The tier classification + routing foundation is confirmed by 3 consecutive keeps; cycle-5 adds the action layer as the third and final friction reduction. Exploit signal: 3 keeps in a row on the same pattern family.
changes_description: Updated ecosystem_scan_adoption surface to cycle-5 format: each actionable-now routing tag now paired with a specific 1-sentence suggested action (e.g., '→ dev: evaluate agentmemory repo as cortextOS memory layer replacement'). Previous cycle-4 format had tier labels + routing destination only — cycle-5 adds the concrete next-action layer. actionable-within-30d items retain routing tag only (no action), watch-only items unchanged. Goal: convert routing-primed recipients into single-step execution, targeting >9 adoptions (above cycle-2 high).
